info.title: Verdocs Platform API
info.description: The Verdocs Platform API provides endpoints for performing all common operations in the Verdocs Platform. API endpoints follow REST conventions and use standard HTTP methods for most operations. All calls are made via a common endpoint [https://api.verdocs.com](https://api.verdocs.com) and use JSON formatting for both request and response bodies.

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: An Organization represents a logical workspace that groups users, documents, and resources under a shared administrative domain. Organizations enable multi-tenant management by isolating data and permissions between different teams or companies. Each organization typically contains its own users, templates, envelopes, and settings, ensuring that activity and access remain scoped to that organization. Organizations are commonly used to support multiple teams, departments, or clients within a single platform.

tags.name: Envelopes
tags.description: An envelope is a digital package that groups one or more documents together with their signing instructions, recipients, and metadata for the purpose of routing, signing, and tracking the documents in an electronic signature workflow.

tags.name: Envelope Documents
tags.description: The files that need to be signed or reviewed (PDFs, contracts, etc.), within an envelope.

tags.name: Signatures and Initials
tags.description: Signatures & Initials are the visual marks applied by recipients to indicate approval or acknowledgment of a document. A signature represents a recipient’s full legal signature, while an initial is typically used to confirm review of specific sections or pages. In the Verdocs platform, signatures and initials are placed within designated signing fields in a document. When a recipient completes a signing step, the platform captures the signature or initials along with metadata such as the timestamp, signer identity, and audit trail information.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: API keys are required when using Verdocs technpologies. Please log in to your account to view/generate your API Key.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Webhooks are HTTP callbacks that notify external systems when specific events occur within the platform. Common events include document signing completion, envelope status changes, or recipient actions. Webhooks enable real-time integration with external applications, allowing automated workflows, notifications, and auditing.

tags.name: Fields
tags.description: Fields are interactive elements placed within a document that collect or display information during the signing process. Fields define where recipients must provide input or review information. Common field types include signature fields, initials, text inputs, dates, checkboxes, and dropdown selections. Fields can be assigned to specific recipients and may include validation rules, default values, or formatting constraints. Fields ensure that documents capture the required information and guide recipients through the signing workflow.

tags.name: Roles
tags.description: Roles define the permissions and responsibilities assigned to users or recipients within the system. Roles determine what actions a user can perform, such as creating envelopes, approving documents, managing templates, or accessing administrative settings. Assigning roles ensures that users only have access to the resources and actions appropriate for their responsibilities.

tags.name: Template Documents
tags.description: A Template Document is a single file included within a template that defines part of the content and structure for a signing workflow. Template documents can contain predefined fields, placeholders, and formatting that standardize the signing process. When a template is used to create an envelope, each template document is included in the signing transaction according to the template configuration.

tags.name: Templates
tags.description: A Template is a reusable document configuration that defines the structure of a signing workflow. Templates typically include one or more documents, predefined fields, recipient roles, and routing rules. By using templates, organizations can standardize frequently used agreements and automate document preparation. When a template is used, the platform generates a new signing transaction based on the template’s configuration, allowing documents to be sent for signature quickly and consistently.

tags.name: Profiles
tags.description: A Profile represents the personal or account information of a user within the platform. Profiles store data such as the user’s name, email, contact information, and preferences. Profiles are used to identify users in signing workflows, manage access, and personalize communications or notifications.

x-codeSamples.label: CURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.verdocs.com/v2/organization-invitations/accept" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN" \
    -H "content-type: application/json" \
    -d '{"param": "value"}'

x-codeSamples.label: NodeJS
Ruby
x-codeSamples.source:
require 'uri'
require 'net/http'
require 'openssl'

url = URI("https://api.verdocs.com/v2/organization-invitations/accept")

http = Net::HTTP.new(url.host, url.port)
http.use_ssl = true

request = Net::HTTP::Post.new(url)
request["content-type"] = 'application/json'
request["Authorization"] = 'Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN'

response = http.request(request)
puts response.read_body

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import http.client

conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection("api.verdocs.com")
headers = {
    'content-type': "application/json",
    'Authorization': "Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN"
}

conn.request("POST", "/v2/organization-invitations/accept", headers=headers)

res = conn.getresponse()
data = res.read()
print(data.decode("utf-8"))

x-codeSamples.label: C-Sharp
Java
x-codeSamples.source:
HttpResponse<String> response = Unirest.post("https://api.verdocs.com/v2/organization-invitations/accept")
  .header("content-type", "application/json")
  .header("Authorization", "Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN")
  .asString();

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: Request pre-defined view. `inbox` returns envelopes where action is required by the caller. `sent` returns envelopes created by the caller. `action` returns envelopes where action is required by the caller. `waiting` returns envelopes where action is required by anyone. `completed` returns envelopes where all actions are complete.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Match envelopes in one of the specified states.

parameters.name: include_org
parameters.description: If true, include organizations-shared envelopes

parameters.name: template_id
parameters.description: Match envelopes created from the specified template ID

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Match envelopes created before this date

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Match envelopes created after this date

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Return results sorted by this criteria

parameters.name: ascending
parameters.description: Set true/false to override the sort direction. Note that the default depends on `sort_by`. Date-based sorts default to descending, while name and status default to ascending.

parameters.name: rows
parameters.description: Limit the number of rows returned

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specify which page of results to return

count.description: The total number of records matching the query, helpful for pagination

rows.description: The number of rows returned in this response page

post.summary: Add a field to a template

error.description: Description of the error that occurred.
Description of the error that occurred.
Description of the error that occurred.
Description of the error that occurred.

name.description: Name for the new field. Field names must be unique within a template. Although special characters are allowed, they must be URL-encoded in subsequent requests, so it is recommended to use only alphanumeric characters and hyphens if possible.

role_name.description: Role to assign to the field. Role names must be valid, so it is recommended to create roles before fields.

document_id.description: ID of the document upon which to place the field.

page.description: 0-based page number upon which to place the field

x.description: X position for the field (left to right)

y.description: Y position for the field (_bottom to top!_)

label.description: Optional label to display above the field

width.description: Width of the field. Note that all fields have built-in defaults, and it is recommended that this only be set on text fields.

address.description: Pre-fill KBA address for the recipient, if known.

auth_methods.description: To enable authentication for the recipient, set to 'pin' or 'identity'.

city.description: Pre-fill KBA city for the recipient, if known.

delegator.description: Whether the recipient may delegate their tasks to others. Should be false for most standard workflows.

dob.description: Pre-fill KBA date-of-birth for the recipient, if known.

email.description: The email address of the recipient. One of

first_name.description: The name of the recipient as it will be displayed in reports and queries, e.g. 'Paige Turner'.

message.description: A custom message to include in the email or SMS invitation. May be left blank for a default message.

order.description:
The 1-based order within the sequence for the recipient. Recipients at the same sequence act in parallel, so
this is only for display purposes.

passcode.description: If Passcode-based authentication is used, the passcode to challenge the user to enter.

phone.description: The phone number of the recipient. One of

phone_auth.description:
If SMS-based authentication is used, the phone number to which one-time codes should be sent.
NOTE: This may be different from the phone number used for notifications, but leaving it blank
will trigger an error rather than defaulting to the notifications phone number to avoid mistaken
assumptions (e.g. if SMS notifications are not enabled for the organization, but SMS authentication
is).

role_name.description: Unique identifier for the recipient. When using a template, must match one of the pre-defined roles in the template's Recipients list.

sequence.description:
The 1-based sequence number for the recipient. This can be used to override the template's workflow. Recipients
are processed in parallel for each matching sequence number (e.g. all recipients at level "1" may act in parallel)
and in series between sequence numbers (e.g. all recipients at level "1" must complete their tasks before
recipients at level "2" may act).

ssn_last_4.description: Pre-fill KBA SSN-Last-4 for the recipient, if known.

state.description: Pre-fill KBA state for the recipient, if known.

type.description: The type of role to create. Most participants in standard flows will be "signer" recipients.

zip.description: Pre-fill KBA zip for the recipient, if known.

IDocumentFromData.description:
Represents a document to be attached to a template via a Base64-encoded string attachment. This is the best option
for maximum security but there is a 10MB size limit for the entire creation request. Requests attaching larger files
should use

name.description: A name for the attachment.

IDocumentFromUri.description:
Represents a document to be attached to a template via an externally-accessible URI. A copy of the document will be
downloaded from the specified URI. Note that the URI will be accessed without headers or other authorization methods
set, so the URI itself must encode any security tokens or keys required to access the file.

name.description: A name for the attachment.

uri.description: The URI to retrieve the file from.

IEnvelope.description:
An Envelope is a workflow wrapper that shepherds one or more Documents through the various recipients in a signing
process.

canceled_at.description: Date/time when the envelope was canceled, or null.

created_at.description: Date/time when the envelope was created.

data.description:
Storage for arbitrary data that may be used e.g. to track source database/record IDs to relate Envelopes back to
internal systems/applications.

expires_at.description: Date/time when the envelope will automatically expire, or null.

followup_reminders.description: Delay (in seconds) before subsequent remidners are sent (min: 12hrs). Set to 0 or null to disable.

initial_reminder.description: Delay (in seconds) before the first reminder is sent (min: 4hrs). Set to 0 or null to disable.

max_reminder_days.description: Maximum number of days (after envelope creation) for which reminders will be sent. Defaults to 14.

name.description: Name of the envelope. By defaut, inherited from the envelope's template, but may be overridden when the envelope is created.

next_reminder.description: When the next reminder is scheduled to be sent.

no_contact.description: If set to true, no email or SMS messages will be sent to any of the envelope's recipients.

organization_id.description: ID of the organization to which the envelope belongs.

sender_email.description: Override the sender "email address" in places like the Certificate.

sender_name.description: Override the sender "name" in places like the Certificate.

signed.description: If true, the envelope is fully submitted, processed, certificate-generated, and all documents have been stamped and signed.

status.description: Current status of the envelope. Note that 'complete', 'declined', and 'canceled' are immutable/permanent end states. Also, 'complete' does NOT mean "fully signed in all aspects" (see "signed" for that). 'complete' means complete from a user's perspective: all required data has been submitted and workflow steps completed.

template_id.description: ID of the template from which the envelope was created.

updated_at.description: Date/time when the envelope was created.

visibility.description: Defaults to 'private'. If set to 'shared', this envelope will be visible to other users in the same organization. Ignored for personal profiles.

IEnvelopeDocument.description: An EnvelopeDocument is an individual document inside an Envelope package.

created_at.description: Date/time when the document was created.

name.description: The name of the document

order.description: The order in which the document appears in the envelope.

page_sizes.description: Collection of width/height dimensions for each page

signed.description: Whether the file has been signed. Note that document.signed is different from envelope.signed. Documents are signed first, then the certificate is created and signed, then the envelope is finally marked signed.

template_document_id.description: Unique identifier for the template (UUID). May be null for envelopes created without templates

type.description: Whether the document is a signer-supplied attachment or a Verdocs-generated certificate

updated_at.description: Date/time when the document was created.

default.description: The default value for the field.

document_id.description: The ID of the document the field is for.

envelope_id.description: The ID of the envelope the field is for.

group.description: For fields that support grouping (radio buttons and check boxes) the value selected will be stored under this name.

label.description: If set, the placeholder/label for the field.

multiline.description: For text boxes, allows more than one line of text to be entered.

name.description: The machine name of the field, e.g.

options.description: For dropdowns, the options that are selectable.

page.description: The 1-based page number the field is displayed on. "Self-placed" fields that the user must apply will be on page 0.

placeholder.description: The placeholder to show in the field.

prepared.description: Not sent by the server. Used in the UI to identify prepared fields.

readonly.description: If true, the field will be not be editable by the participant(s). NOTE: Fields may not be both required and readonly.

required.description: If true, the field will be required

role_name.description: The ID of the role in the recipients list, e.g.

x.description: The X position of the field.

y.description: The Y position of the field.

checked.description: For checkboxes, whether it is currently checked

id.description: The unique ID of the field

selected.description: For radio buttons, whether it is currently selected

value.description: The visible label for the field e.g. 'Not Applicable'

x.description: The X position of the field on the page. Self-placed fields will have an X value of 0.

y.description: The Y position of the field on the page. Self-placed fields will have an X value of 0.

base64.description: Signatures and Initials, result will be "signed"

result.description: If the field has been filled in, this contains the current value

ascending.description: Set to true or false to control the sort order. Omit this field to sort dates descending, names ascending.

is_creator.description: List only those templates created by the caller.

is_starred.description: List only those templates with at least one "star".

q.description: List only those templates whose names, descriptions, etc contain this search term.

rows.description: Number of rows to retrieve. Defaults to 10.

visibility.description: Visibility status of templates to include. private_shared is the default (private + shared)

access_key.description:
The access key that matches the signing session. May be used for later initiation requests
if in-person signing was desired, but not necessarily instant (e.g. to hand-off to a
companion application. **NOTE: Access keys are as sensitive as Bearer Tokens and must be
protected from theft and unauthorized sharing!**

access_token.description:
An access token that may be used with a VerdocsEndpoint to perform signing operations.
When signing, the caller's "authentication" status will be recorded as "in-person".

envelope.description:
A copy of the envelope related to the signing session. This is almost always needed when
a signing session is being started, so it is included here for convenience.

link.description: A valid Verdocs Web URL that hosts a signing experience.

recipient.description:
A copy of the recipient record related to the signing session. This is almost always needed when
a signing session is being started, so it is included here for convenience.

contact_email.description: If the organization is a business, its name. Note that a business name can be different from an organization name.

id.description: The unique ID of the organization

name.description: The organization's name.

current.description: If true, this is the caller's "currently selected" profile. All operations will performed "as" this profile.

id.description: The unique ID of the profile

organization_id.description: The profile's organization ID, or a global "Verdocs" organization that all personal profiles are members of.

user_id.description:
In Verdocs, a user may have multiple profiles. A user represents a single person. A profile
represents that person within an organization. Some profiles may have no user atached, typically
Contacts and Signers. This can change if that person registers for a user later.

address.description: Street address. Only used in KBA workflows. Combine two-line addresses into a single string.

auth_method_states.description: The status of each auth method enabled.

auth_methods.description: The types of authentication/verification required for this recipient.

auth_step.description: The next verification step that must be performed.

city.description: Zip code. Only used in KBA workflows.

disclosures.description: The disclosure text the recipient accepted.

disclosures_accepted_at.description: Date/time the recipient agreed to their e-signing disclosures.

dob.description: Date of birth. Only used in KBA workflows.

id.description: Used only by the Web SDK during builder processes. Not stored in the backend.

in_app_key.description:
Only returned in creation/getEnvelopeById requests by the creator. May be used for in-person signing. Note that
signing sessions started with this key will be marked as "In App" authenticated. For higher authentication levels,
e.g. email, the signer must follow a link send via the appropriate channel (email).

kba_questions.description:
If a KBA step requires the user to answer a challenge/differentiator question, the
question(s) to ask.

order.description:
The order indicates the order in which recipients are listed in a single "level" of the workflow. Note that
recipients at the same level may act in parallel despite this value. However, it can often be useful to visually
arrange recipients to match related business processes so this field allows for that.

passcode.description:
If auth_method is set to "passcode" this is the passcode required. For security reasons, this
field will only be visible to the creator of the envelope.

sequence.description:
The sequence number indicates the order in which Recipients act. Multiple recipients may have the same sequence
number, in which case they may act in parallel. (e.g. all Recipients at sequence 2 will receive invites once
all Recipients at sequence 1 have signed.)

ssn_last_4.description: Last 4 digits of SSN. Only used in KBA workflows.

state.description: Zip code. Only used in KBA workflows.

zip.description: Zip code. Only used in KBA workflows.

IRecipientKbaStepChallenge.description:
If a positive identification was not possible, the user may be asked to answer 1 or more
challenge questions via this response. They should be submitted via

IRecipientKbaStepComplete.description: KBA has been completed and no further action is required.

IRecipientKbaStepFailed.description:
Identity verification has failed. The user should be shown the message included. No further
signing operations may be performed.

IRecipientKbaStepIdentity.description:
A full identity verification is required. Prompt the user for their address and other (optional)
details and submit them via

IRecipientKbaStepNone.description: KBA is not required at this time.

IRecipientKbaStepPin.description: A PIN code is required. Prompt the user to enter this PIN, and submit it via

IRole.description:
A placeholder for an individual recipient, CC, or other party in a signing flow. Roles may be "known" or "unknown."
"Known" roles will have their email address supplied in the template which will get copied to envelopes created from
it. This is used when a certain party will always be the same, e.g. a leasing agent counter-signing a lease.
"Unknown" roles are dynamic, and will be filled in later when the envelope is created.

id.description: Used only by the Web SDK during builder processes. Not stored in the backend.

name_locked.description: If set, the recipient will not be allowed to change their legal name.

order.description:
The order indicates the order in which recipients are listed in a single "level" of the workflow. Note that
recipients at the same level may act in parallel despite this value. However, it can often be useful to visually
arrange recipients to match related business processes so this field allows for that.

sequence.description:
The sequence number indicates the order in which Roles act. Multiple roles may have the same sequence
number, in which case they may act in parallel. (e.g. all Roles at sequence 2 will receive invites once
all Recipients at sequence 1 have signed.)

access_token.description:
An access token that may be used with a VerdocsEndpoint to perform signing operations.
When signing, the caller's "authentication" status will be recorded as "in-person".

envelope.description:
A copy of the envelope related to the signing session. This is almost always needed when
a signing session is being started, so it is included here for convenience.

initials.description:
Stored initials blocks for the recipient. All entries are returned to support workflows in
which that is required, but for most use-cases, only the first entry should be used.

recipient.description:
A copy of the recipient record related to the signing session. This is almost always needed when
a signing session is being started, so it is included here for convenience.

signatures.description:
Stored signature blocks for the recipient. All entries are returned to support workflows in
which that is required, but for most use-cases, only the first entry should be used.

ISigningSession.description: A Signing Session connects a caller to a role within an envelope, and can be used only for calls related to signing that envelope.

ITemplate.description:
A reusable template for creating signable instruments. Templates are used to create Envelopes which contain
Documents to sign.

counter.description: Number of times the template has been used.

data.description:
Storage for arbitrary data that may be used e.g. to track source database/record IDs to relate Templates back to
internal systems/applications.

followup_reminders.description: Delay (in seconds) before subsequent remidners are sent (min: 12hrs). Set to 0 or null to disable.

id.description: The unique ID of the template.

initial_reminder.description: Delay (in seconds) before the first reminder is sent (min: 4hrs). Set to 0 or null to disable.

is_public.description:
If true, the template is visible publicly. Note that this does not necessarily mean it is also visible to the
user's organization. It may be desirable to create documents that are public but that do not appear in the
organization's shared templates list. To achieve this, set both

is_sendable.description: If true, the template is considered "sendable" (it has at least one signer, and every signer has at least one field.)

last_used_at.description: Last-used date/time (when the template was used to create a document).

max_reminder_days.description: Maximum number of days (after envelope creation) for which reminders will be sent. Defaults to 14.

sender.description:
Who will "own" envelopes created from this template. Note that while either option is
technically allowed for all visibility settings, "template_owner" only has an effect if
visibility is "shared" or "public".

star_counter.description: Number of times the template has been "starred".

visibility.description: If set, the visibility level for the template.

itemId.description: The item ID of the source file.

name.description: Name for the template to create.

oboToken.description:
On-Behalf-Of access token generated for the request. This must have an audience of "https://graph.microsoft.com"
with Read access to the source file. This token is used ephemerally and discarded after the request, but it is
still recommended that you generate it with the minimal permissions possible.

siteId.description: The site ID the source file is in.

description.description: Optional description for the template to help identify it.

documents.description:
Optional list of roles to create. Documents are required if roles or fields will also be specified. Files may
be attached via a number of methods (browser File object, remote URI reference, or Base64-encoded string) but
all entries must of of the same type. If browser File objects are provided, the request will use a FORM POST
call, otherwise it will use traditional XHR.

fields.description: Optional list of fields to create.

followup_reminders.description: Delay (in seconds) before subsequent remidners are sent (min: 12hrs). Set to 0 or null to disable.

initial_reminder.description: Delay (in seconds) before the first reminder is sent (min: 4hrs). Set to 0 or null to disable.

is_public.description:
Optional (defaults to false). Public templates may be found (via search) and viewed by anyone.
@deprecated. See visibility.

name.description: Name for the template to create.

roles.description: Optional list of roles to create. Note that if roles are not included in the request, fields will be ignored.

sender.description: Optional (defaults to EVERYONE_AS_CREATOR). Who may create and send envelopes using this template.

visibility.description: If set, the visibility level for the template.

ITemplateDocument.description: A file attached to the template for display/signing.

default.description: The default value for the field.

document_id.description: The ID of the document the field is for.

group.description: For fields that support grouping (radio buttons and check boxes) the value selected will be stored under this name.

multiline.description: For text boxes, allows more than one line of text to be entered.

name.description: The machine name of the field, e.g.

options.description: For dropdowns, the options that are selectable.

placeholder.description: The placeholder to show in the field.

readonly.description: If true, the field will be not be editable by the participant(s). NOTE: Fields may not be both required and readonly.

role_name.description: The ID of the role in the recipients list, e.g.

template_id.description: The ID of the template the field is for.

x.description: The X position of the field.

y.description: The Y position of the field.

action.description: Trigger a reminder invite, or fully reset the recipient's status.

address.description: If KBA-based authentication is used, the recipient's address to prefill. May only be changed if the recipient has not already completed KBA-based auth.

city.description: If KBA-based authentication is used, the recipient's city to prefill. May only be changed if the recipient has not already completed KBA-based auth.

dob.description: If KBA-based authentication is used, the recipient's date of birth to prefill. May only be changed if the recipient has not already completed KBA-based auth.

email.description: The email address of the recipient. If changed, a new invite will be sent.

first_name.description: The name of the recipient as it will be displayed in reports and queries, e.g. 'Paige Turner'.

message.description: A custom message to include in the email or SMS invitation. May be left blank for a default message.

name_locked.description: If set, the recipient will not be allowed to change their legal name.

passcode.description: If Passcode-based authentication is used, the passcode to challenge the user to enter. May only be changed if the recipient has not already completed passcode-based auth.

phone.description: The phone number of the recipient. If changed, a new invite will be sent.

ssn_last_4.description: If KBA-based authentication is used, the recipient's SSN-Last-4 to prefill. May only be changed if the recipient has not already completed KBA-based auth.

state.description: If KBA-based authentication is used, the recipient's state to prefill. May only be changed if the recipient has not already completed KBA-based auth.

zip.description: If KBA-based authentication is used, the recipient's zip code to prefill. May only be changed if the recipient has not already completed KBA-based auth.

IUserSession.description: A User Session connects a caller to a Verdocs profile, and can be used for any operations that profile may perform.

persist.description: By default, sessions will be persisted to localStorage. Set

TAccountPermissionAdminAdd.description: Grant the "admin" role to other organization members.

TAccountPermissionAdminRemove.description: Remove the "admin" role from other organization members.

TAccountPermissionMemberAdd.description: Grant the "member" role to other organization members.

TAccountPermissionMemberRemove.description: Remove the "member" role from other organization members.

TAccountPermissionMemberView.description: View the members of an organization.

TAccountPermissionOwnerAdd.description: Grant the "owner" role to other organization members.

TAccountPermissionOwnerRemove.description: Remove the "owner" role from other organization members.

TActiveSession.description: An active authenticated session within Verdocs, either for signing or regular user-based operations.

TEnvelopePermissionCancel.description:
Cancel envelopes. This is a default permission for most users, but it may be removed for
highly regulated environments where envelope activities must be audited, and should not
be canceled.

TEnvelopePermissionOrg.description:
View envelopes created by other members of the same organization. By default, only templates
having sharing settings controlled by their creators. Envelopes are usually private to the
callers who created them. In some organizations it may be useful to have users who can see
"all activity" by all organization members. This is particularly useful when applied to API
keys to develop applications that can access all data across the organization.

TEnvelopePermissionView.description:
View envelopes. This is a default permission for most users, but it may be removed for
highly regulated environments where once sent, envelopes may only be viewed by specific
users.

TOrgPermissionTransfer.description:
Transfer ownership of the organization. This primarily allows the holder to remove his/her own
Owner role or add new Owners even if the holder is not one themselves. This is primarily intended
to be used for reseller scenarios.

TPermission.description: Operation within Verdocs that users may perform.

TRecipientAuthMethod.description:
The authentication method(s) required for a recipient to access an envelope. "Passcode" will require a
PIN or passcode to be entered, which is intended to be known to the sender and recipient ahead of time
and communicated by means of their choosing. "SMS" and "Email" will send a one-time-code via the respective
channel for the recipient to enter. "KBA" will require the recipient to confirm personal information such
as prior addresses, phone numbers, etc. "ID" will require the recipient to perform full ID-based verification.

TRole.description:
Roles provide access to groups of permissions. Note that for historical reasons there is some overlap in the
use of the term "role". TRole refers to a user type. A "Role" (IRole) is a Template participant placeholder.

TSession.description: Represents a possibly-authenticated session within Verdocs, either for signing or regular user-based operations.

TSessionType.description:
Verdocs supports two types of authenticated sessions: User and Signing. Both behave similarly and have similar
properties, but signing sessions only have access to a small set of signing-related functions.

TSortTemplateBy.description: Plans provide access to Verdocs product features.

TTemplatePermissionCreateOrg.description: Create templates shared with other users of the same organization.

TTemplatePermissionCreatePersonal.description: Create templates private to the creator.

TTemplatePermissionCreatePublic.description:
Create public templates. Public templates are still owned and managed by the creator,
but may be searched for and used to create envelopes by other users.
